diagrams.title: Sequential Access Method
diagrams.description: Shows how records are accessed one after another in order, requiring the read head to move through each record sequentially
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the linear nature of sequential access and understand why it is efficient for batch processing but slow for random access

diagrams.title: Direct/Random Access Method
diagrams.description: Illustrates how any record can be accessed directly by calculating its physical address using starting address and record number
elements: Arrows showing direct jumps to any record
diagrams.purpose: Understand how direct access calculates physical locations enabling O(1) access to any record without traversing preceding records

diagrams.title: Indexed Access Method
diagrams.description: Depicts the index block containing pointers to all data blocks, showing how index is searched to locate desired data
elements: Index block with pointers (P0, P1, P2...PN)
Arrows from index to data blocks
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how the index acts as a lookup table enabling direct access to any data block through a two-step process: read index, then read data

diagrams.title: File Allocation Methods Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows three allocation methods: Contiguous (blocks together), Linked (blocks with pointers), and Indexed (blocks with index block)
elements: Free blocks contiguous in free space but fragmented
diagrams.purpose: Compare how different allocation methods store file blocks on disk and understand their implications for access methods and fragmentation
